supporters.name: Tim Fairfax Family Foundation
supporters.tone: warm, place-based, story-rich
supporters.framing_notes_excerpt: Tim Fairfax loves regional and community-led. Lead with Room 3 (Mounty Yarns, PICC, Oonchiumpa) and the touring model. The container-as-method is a creative-practice claim that lands well here.

supporters.name: The Smith Family
supporters.tone: evidence-led, educational outcomes
supporters.framing_notes_excerpt: Highest-scoring funder in our system. Frame around educational outcomes — 18% of detained kids complete education vs the alternatives' completion rates.

supporters.name: Amnesty International Australia
supporters.tone: rights-based, structurally honest, willing to confront
supporters.framing_notes_excerpt: Amnesty is one of the few funders for whom the confrontational frame is the right pitch. Lead with 21x overrepresentation, then the political-will claim, then the failure rate. They want partners who will name it.

supporters.name: National Indigenous Australians Agency
supporters.tone: procurement-ready, IPP-compliant, enterprise
supporters.framing_notes_excerpt: Procurement audience under the Indigenous Procurement Policy. Lead with the enterprise framing — Goods on Country is an IPP-eligible community-owned enterprise, not a social program.

supporters.name: Allbirds
supporters.tone: supply-chain-honest, materials-curious
supporters.framing_notes_excerpt: B-Corp with materials-science DNA. Stretch Bed material story (HDPE, canvas, galvanised steel) is a natural conversation. Smaller funder, but a credible co-sponsor for the tour.

supporters.name: Who Gives A Crap

supporters.framing_notes_excerpt: Australian B-Corp that built a business out of dignified essentials. Goods on Country is a sister category. Tour co-sponsor candidate. Their 50% donation model maps to the Goods enterprise model conceptually.
